description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring green and dark gray patches across its fuselage and wings. The aircraft has a low-wing configuration with straight wings and rounded tips, and red star insignias are visible on the wings and tail, suggesting Soviet or Russian origin. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, while the nose houses two propeller engines. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for historical or wartime urban dioramas.
description: A twin-propeller, camouflage-painted military airplane with red star insignias, low straight wings, and a conventional tail. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing both engines, the left wing, and the tail with red star insignia.
description_view_1: Rear side view highlighting the right wing, tail section, and both main landing gear wheels.
description_view_2: Side profile view emphasizing the straight wing, cockpit canopy, and the red star on the fuselage and tail.
description_view_3: Front three-quarter view displaying both propeller engines, cockpit canopy, and the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with rounded tips and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the leading edge of each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely multi-seat based on canopy length.
